channel.title: CyberNetSec - Threat Actor
channel.description: Latest threat actor threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has discovered at least 18 malicious browser extensions masquerading as Generative AI productivity tools. These extensions, some downloaded by thousands, are not enhancing productivity but are instead functioning as Remote Access Trojans (RATs), infostealers, and search h...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
item.description:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the notorious BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware gang. Martino abused his position at a crypto brokerage firm, using his insider knowledge of his clients' negotiating strategies and insurance limits to help BlackCat maxim...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: TeamPCP Weaponizes npm with Malicious Bitwarden CLI in Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack targeting the npm ecosystem has been uncovered by Unit 42, attributed to the threat actor group TeamPCP. The attackers published a malicious package, `@bitwarden/cli` version `2026.4.0`, which perfectly impersonates the legitimate Bitwarden command-line tool. Upon...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
item.description:
A rare public conflict has erupted between two rival ransomware groups, 0APT and KryBit. The feud, which played out between March 28 and April 12, 2026, involved both groups hacking each other and leaking sensitive operational data. 0APT initiated the spat by leaking KryBit's admin panel. KryBit ret...

Severity: Low

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data
item.description:
Medical device manufacturer Medtronic has confirmed it sustained a cybersecurity incident following claims by the ShinyHunters threat group. The group alleged it stole over 9 million records and terabytes of corporate data, issuing a 'pay or leak' ultimatum. Medtronic stated the breach was contained...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion
item.description:
The ShinyHunters cybercrime group has publicly leaked a database containing 1.4 million records from the online learning platform Udemy after a 'pay or leak' extortion attempt failed. The compromised data is extensive, including user and instructor PII, contact information, and sensitive financial p...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Flaw Means Paying the Ransom is Pointless—Large Files are Wiped Forever
item.description:
Security researchers at Check Point have uncovered a critical design flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes it to permanently destroy files larger than 128 KB instead of encrypting them. This bug, present in the Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants, discards essential decryption keys (nonces) duri...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: ShinyHunters Ransomware Spree: Carnival, Zara's Parent, and 40+ Firms Breached in Massive Campaign
item.description:
The ShinyHunters ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a large-scale attack campaign that has compromised more than 40 organizations worldwide. The victims, listed on the group's data leak site, span the retail, insurance, and hospitality sectors. High-profile targets include Carnival Corp...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: China-Based Silver Fox APT Expands Espionage Campaign Across Asia with Fake Tax Audits
item.description:
The China-based threat group Silver Fox has launched a new wave of attacks targeting businesses and individuals across Asia. The group, which has been active since at least 2022, uses fake tax audit notifications and counterfeit software update alerts to deliver malware. According to analysis by S2W...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: GlassWorm Campaign Evolves, Uses Zig-Based Dropper to Infect All Developer IDEs
item.description:
The ongoing GlassWorm cyber-espionage campaign has adopted a new, sophisticated tool: a dropper written in the Zig programming language. This new malware component was discovered hidden within a malicious Open VSX extension masquerading as a legitimate WakaTime activity tracker. The dropper's primar...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Supply Chain Attack, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →
